mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or inflation-adjusted returns show no significant decline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signal weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary risks that drive S&P 500 forward returns negative over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with combined INDPRO/GDPC1 declines, or sector-specific supply shocks offset aggregate trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 4% during high consumer spending (RSAFS) signals stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE > 4.0 and RSAFS > 700,000, or real wage growth outpaces inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, or employment gains fail to translate to sectoral earnings.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) amid rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals demand-side imbalance, creating mixed S&P 500 forward return signals over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST < 1350 and RSAFS > 690,000, or housing data fails to predict sector rotations.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with disinflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) creates a 'Goldilocks' environment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL tightness, or bond yields diverge from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) coupled with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals shifting demand patterns, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS > 690,000 and HOUST < 1350, or housing data fails to predict equity sector rotations.
